title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
author: Mark Twain
content: “All right, then, I'll go to hell.”

title: The Fall of the House of Usher
author: Edgar Allan Poe
content: “I feel that the period will sooner or later arrive when I must abandon life and reason together, in some struggle with the grim phantasm, Fear.”
action: Name your fears to disarm them.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
content: “It is not down in any map; true places never are.”

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
content: “So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.”
action: Focus on the destination, not the drag.

title: East of Eden
author: John Steinbeck
content: “Timshel—‘Thou mayest.’ It does not say you must. It does not say you shall. It says you may.”
action: You have the power to choose.
